nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (Negative Prompt)
widgets_values:
Animation Prompt: Farmer Pitchfork Slash Attack (with White Slash Effect) - SILENT VERSION
Character Details (REVISED - Based on Initial Pose):

Robust, bearded farmer character in brown/tan attire (brown hat, tan shirt, brown pants, boots)
Determined, focused expression with forward gaze
Eyes lock onto target direction with concentrated look
Head rotates with attacking motion, slight upward angle during slash
Both arms engaged in powerful gripping and slashing motion
Overall posture: starts from upright ready stance (as shown in image), transitions to powerful attacking position
Mouth in natural, relaxed resting state - closed and composed
Silent, concentrated demeanor throughout entire animation
Quiet, focused presence with composed facial features (no shouting expression)
Backpack visible on back - remains stable during attack motion

Animation Motion:

Primary action: Powerful diagonal pitchfork slash with full-body rotation and extension from neutral ready stance
Arc range: Pitchfork swings in controlled 120-150 degree arcs from ready position to extended strike
Phase 1 (0-20%): Windup/chamber - pitchfork drawn back to shoulder height from neutral waist position, torso coils, weight shifts to back leg, arms bend for tension
Phase 2 (20-45%): Acceleration - explosive drive forward and downward, torso rotates powerfully, shoulders twist, hips drive rotation, arms extend with force
Phase 3 (45-70%): Slash contact - pitchfork at full extension, tines slice through target arc, maximum reach achieved, body at peak power, full rotation complete
Phase 4 (70-85%): Follow-through - arms continue motion slightly past contact point, momentum carries body forward, shoulders complete rotation, stance widens for stability
Phase 5 (85-100%): Recovery - arms settle back, torso straightens, weight redistributes, returns to ready position matching initial pose shown in image
Easing: Ease-out on windup (tension build), ease-out on acceleration (explosive power), linear on contact (impact), ease-in on recovery (settling)
Secondary motion: Shoulder blade engagement, hip rotation driving power, slight knee bend for stability, head rotation following body twist, backpack slight bounce
Weight: Powerful, dynamic motion - attack feels forceful and impactful, full-body rotation generates authentic strike power, grounded stance maintained

Pitchfork Prop Animation:

Phase 1 (0-20%): Pitchfork held at waist/neutral ready position (as in initial image), begins movement upward and backward, gripped firmly in both hands, tines face forward
Phase 2 (20-45%): Pitchfork accelerates backward and upward to shoulder chamber position, handle rotates, tines angle upward, preparation for forward strike
Phase 3 (45-70%): Pitchfork drives forward and downward in powerful arc, tines lead motion, tool extends to maximum reach, handle aligned with body rotation
Phase 4 (70-85%): Pitchfork continues arc motion, tines trail through air slightly past strike point, momentum still carries forward motion
Phase 5 (85-100%): Pitchfork returns to ready/neutral waist position matching initial pose, reset for potential follow-up or loop continuation
Motion: Integrated, powerful prop movement - pitchfork acts as weapon extension of farmer's body, tool moves with full body rotation and weight distribution
Final state: Pitchfork cycles back to starting ready position (waist height) as shown in initial image

White Slash Effect Animation:

Effect timing: WHITE SLASH EFFECT occurs during Phase 3 (45-70%) at peak contact/extension moment
Effect position: Follows pitchfork tines through arc path, trails behind tines creating slash line
Effect appearance: Bright white/light cyan slashing lines following tine path, 3-4 parallel lines matching pitchfork tine spacing
Effect intensity: Maximum brightness at Phase 3 (55-60% - contact moment), fades in during Phase 2 (30-45%), fades out during Phase 4 (75-85%)
Effect trail: Glowing white arc trail follows tines from chamber position to full extension, creating dynamic slash motion line
Effect particles: Small white energy particles scatter and radiate at contact point (Phase 3 peak), burst outward from strike location
Effect blur: Motion blur effect on white lines showing high velocity strike motion
Effect duration: ~0.5-0.7 seconds total, peaks at contact moment
Color specification: Bright white (#FFFFFF) to light cyan (#E0FFFF) gradient, high saturation and brightness for impact visibility
Effect layers: Multiple overlapping white slash lines for depth and powerful impact feel
Effect glow: Bright white glow bloom around slash lines for visual impact emphasis

Additional Details:

Lighting: Dynamic, dramatic outdoor farm lighting with shadow casting from strike motion
Color palette: Brown/tan base (matching character clothes) with warm earth tones contrasted with bright white slash effect (high saturation contrast for visibility)
Facial animation: Eyes intense and focused forward, determined expression maintained, occasional blink suppressed during attack, mouth stays closed and still
Mouth/lip animation: Mouth closed in relaxed neutral position throughout entire animation - completely silent and stationary
Facial expression: Determined, focused, quiet demeanor - face remains composed and peaceful, no shouting or exertion facial expression, no lip movement
Mouth movement: Zero mouth/jaw movement - lips remain still and unified in natural resting state (no grunting sounds, no battle cries)
Beard: Slight movement following head rotation and body dynamics, natural bristle flow with motion
Posture: Starts upright from ready stance (as in image), transitions to powerful attacking position, returns to ready stance at end
Hat: Remains secure on head, slight rotation with head motion but stays in place
Backpack: Slight bounce/sway synchronized with torso rotation, remains on back throughout animation
Impact moment: Phase 3 (55-60%) shows maximum extension and power, white effect peaks at this moment with maximum brightness
Sound cues (optional audio markers): Whoosh sound at Phase 2-3 transition, impact/slash sound at Phase 3 peak, settle sound at Phase 5 recovery
Duration: 0.7-1.0 seconds for complete attack animation cycle

Technical Requirements:

Loop point: NOT a seamless loop (transition/action animation, single attack strike)
Start frame: Ready stance with pitchfork at waist/neutral position matching initial image pose
End frame: Ready stance with pitchfork returned to neutral waist position (ready for next action)
Include transition windows for blending to standing idle, running, or next attack
Optimized for game engine integration (mobile/console performance, pixel art compatible)
Reference poses: Ready/start position (waist level), chamber position (shoulder height), peak extension position, recovery position
Include T-pose and combat-ready reference pose for implementation
Foreground/background render separation for UI flexibility
Normal maps and roughness maps for material definition (clothing texture, tool, skin, hat)
Facial rig: Mouth remains in neutral closed position, zero lip-sync or phoneme animation, no jaw movement, no combat expressions
Mouth constraint: Lips locked in resting state - no phonemes, no visemes, no speech animation, no battle cry expressions
Pitchfork rigging: Tool must follow realistic swing physics, collision detection at contact point (Phase 3), smooth arc motion
Backpack rigging: Backpack remains secure, slight physics-based bounce following torso rotation
Hat rigging: Hat stays on head, rotates with head movement, secure attachment
White slash effect specifications:

Layer 1: Main bright white (#FFFFFF) slash line following tine path
Layer 2: Secondary cyan (#E0FFFF) glow layer slightly offset for depth and glow effect
Layer 3: Tertiary lighter white layer for additional intensity and bloom
Particle system: 20-40 white energy particles radiating from contact point at peak
Motion blur: Applied to slash lines showing high velocity strike
Fade curve: Fade-in 30-45%, sustain 45-65%, fade-out 65-85%
Glow bloom: Bright white glow around slash lines (bloom radius ~2-4 pixels for impact)


Optional: Variant intensities (light quick slash, medium power slash, heavy overhead slash)
Optional: Variant directions (horizontal side slash, diagonal down slash, diagonal up slash, overhead downward slash)
Optional environmental details: Screen shake on impact (slight), dust particles accompanying slash effect, ground disturbance at contact zone
Performance optimized: Effect particle count scalable for different hardware, shader-based glow for efficiency
Pixel art compatible: Effect should work with sprite-based rendering and pixel-perfect aesthetics
